steps.title: Identify the key constraint
steps.content: The passage establishes that bumblebees are “among the relatively few wild-bee genera” with social behaviors and dietary generalism—traits associated with increased resilience. This makes bumblebees atypical among wild bees.

steps.title: Infer what Ghisbain would logically argue
steps.content:
Since bumblebees have distinctive traits that most other wild bees lack, and these traits influence how they respond to environmental stress, bumblebees cannot serve as a universal model for wild-bee behavior.

Ghisbain therefore contends that researchers must exercise caution when generalizing from bumblebee studies to wild-bee population declines in general—precisely the position D articulates.

The other choices either contradict established facts in the passage or introduce claims the passage does not support.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
